text:
Emma Raducanuhas raised eyebrows by signing up for a tournament that is set to take place in the same week as the tennis event at the2024 Olympic Gamesin Paris. The British No. 5 turned down the opportunity to compete at the Olympics this summer as she seeks to leave her injury woes in the back mirror by managing her workload.

Raducanu also didn't feature at theFrench Openas she aimed her full focus towards the grass court season andWimbledon, her home Grand Slam.

She reached the semi-finals at the Nottingham Open earlier this month before losing in three sets against British No. 1 and eventual championKatie Boulter.

Raducanu has carried her form into her final Wimbledon preparations at Eastbourne, beating American Sloane Stephens 6-4 6-0 in the first round.

The former US Open champion followed that up with a monumental victory against second seed Jessica Pegula, coming from behind to triumph 4-6 7-5 7-5.

It was her first win against a top-10 player as she continued to build momentum ahead of her latest campaign at SW19.

But Raducanu's rich vein of form has intensified scrutiny over his Olympics absence, as has her decision to enter the Mubadala Citi DC Open, which will run from July 27 to August 4.

That's the same week at the tennis event at Roland Garros, where Boulter will represent Team GB in the women's singles.

An Emma-zing addition this year ??????2021 US Open champion@EmmaRaducanureturns to the#MubadalaCitiDCOpen!pic.twitter.com/KK99I4gdHm

Jack Draper, Cam Norrie, Dan Evans, and Andy Murray will play in the men's singles, while Neal Skupski and Joe Salisbury are Team GB's men's doubles entrants.

Following her first-round win over Stephens in Eastbourne, Raducanu cited her eagerness to avoid the reoccurrence of past injury issues as her reason to reject an Olympics spot.

"I think at one point I was chasing, playing too many tournaments when I wasn't ready," Raducanu explained after her victory against Stephens.

"And then I was just picking up niggle after niggle in every tournament because I never really gave myself any time to do the training and the work. I think that's something I did a lot better this year.

"Even though I might get challenged or questioned for not playing certain tournaments like the French Open or the Olympics, I think that for me, that is just part of it.

"Doing things at my own speed and doing things how I want to rather than how everyone else thinks is best for me.

We use your sign-up to provide content in ways you've consented to and to improve our understanding of you. This may include adverts from us and 3rd parties based on our understanding. You can unsubscribe at any time. Read ourPrivacy Policy

"Because ultimately me and the close few people around me just only know what is actually best for me and my game."

While comments have only fuelled scepticism about her reasoning, it goes without saying that the Olympics would be far more mentally and physically demanding than the tournament in the United States.

title: Emma Raducanu signs up to event same week as Paris Olympics after snubbing Games | Tennis | Sport | Express.co.uk
author: Alex Turk
description: Emma Raducanu will be in action during Paris Olympics week despite snubbing Team GB.
